2 large sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into 2cm cubes
4 cups fresh spinach leaves
½ red onion, thinly sliced
½ cup roasted walnuts, roughly chopped
¼ cup dried cranberries
½ cup crumbled goat cheese or feta

For dressing:
3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
1½ tablespoons balsamic vinegar
1 tablespoon maple syrup
1 teaspoon Dijon mustard
Salt and pepper to taste

Method:
Preheat oven to 200°C. Toss sweet potato cubes with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on
baking tray in single layer. Roast for 25-30 minutes until golden and tender, stirring halfway
through cooking time.

While potatoes roast, whisk together dressing ingredients in small bowl until emulsified.
Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper.

In large salad bowl, combine spinach, sliced red onion, and fresh greens. Add roasted sweet
potatoes while still warm. Drizzle dressing over salad and toss gently to combine. Top with
walnuts, cranberries, and crumbled goat cheese.

Serve immediately while potatoes are still slightly warm, or chill before serving.

Sweet Potato Salad

A vibrant autumn salad combining roasted sweet potato, fresh greens, and warm spices.

Ingredients:
2 large sweet potatoes
4 cups baby spinach
½ red onion, finely sliced
¾ cup candied walnuts
¼ cup dried cranberries
¼ cup goat cheese, crumbled
2 tablespoons pumpkin seeds

Dressing:
¼ cup extra virgin olive oil
2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar
½ tablespoon maple syrup
¼ teaspoon Dijon mustard
Pinch of smoked paprika
Salt and pepper

Preparation:
Roast sweet potato cubes at 200°C for 30 minutes until tender. Prepare dressing by whisking
oil, vinegar, maple syrup, and mustard together. Combine spinach and thinly sliced onion in
large bowl. Add warm sweet potatoes, drizzle with dressing, and toss to combine. Finish with
walnuts, cranberries, and crumbled cheese.

Serves: 4
Time: 35 minutes
